turns.content:
I'd be happy to help you with the current members of the U.S. Supreme Court.

As of my knowledge cutoff in 2023, the current members of the U.S. Supreme Court are:

1. John G. Roberts Jr. - Chief Justice of the United States (appointed by President George W. Bush in 2005)
2. Clarence Thomas - Associate Justice (appointed by President George H.W. Bush in 1991)
3. Samuel A. Alito Jr. - Associate Justice (appointed by President George W. Bush in 2006)
4. Sonia Sotomayor - Associate Justice (appointed by President Barack Obama in 2009)
5. Elena Kagan - Associate Justice (appointed by President Barack Obama in 2010)
6. Neil M. Gorsuch - Associate Justice (appointed by President Donald Trump in 2017)
7. Brett M. Kavanaugh - Associate Justice (appointed by President Donald Trump in 2018)
8. Amy Coney Barrett - Associate Justice (appointed by President Donald Trump in 2020)
9. Ketanji Brown Jackson - Associate Justice (appointed by President Joe Biden in 2022)

Please note that the composition of the Court may change over time due to retirements, resignations, or new appointments. I recommend verifying this information through reputable sources, such as the Supreme Court's official website, the Federal Judicial Center, or other trusted news outlets, to ensure accuracy and up-to-date information.

For citations, you can use the following formats:

* For the Supreme Court's official website: [www.supremecourt.gov](http://www.supremecourt.gov)
* For the Federal Judicial Center: [www.fjc.gov](http://www.fjc.gov)
* For news outlets, such as The New York Times, The Washington Post, or NPR, you can use their online archives or specific articles.

Remember to always verify the accuracy of the information and use reputable sources to maintain the integrity and credibility of your research.

Please note that the composition of the Court may change over time due to retirements, resignations, or new appointments. * For the Federal Judicial Center: [www.fjc.gov](http://www.fjc.gov)
* For news outlets, such as The New York Times, The Washington Post, or NPR, you can use their online archives or specific articles.

Remember to always verify the accuracy of the information and use reputable sources to maintain the integrity and credibility of your research.
